semanticCore: Ravaging, searching through, penetrating homes, raiding, or investigating thoroughly

keyDerivations.meaning: To ravage, search through, enter/invade, or penetrate with violence

figurativeOrRareUsages: بَعَثْنَا عَلَيْكُمْ عِبَادًا لَنَا أُولِي بَأْسٍ شَدِيدٍ فَجَاسُوا خِلَالَ الدِّيَارِ (17:5): We sent against you servants of Ours of great military might, so they ravaged/searched through your homes.
discardedNoise: Jasoos (spy/intelligence agent) - related via the search/spy overlap but distinct.
summaryEn: The root represents penetrating and searching through a space aggressively (Jaws), describing the punitive conquest of Jerusalem where foreign armies invaded and ransacked Jewish homes.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر التغلغل والبحث الشديد داخل الديار والخراب والدخول بطرق القهر والتردد للتفتيش (جوس)، مصوراً اجتياح بابل القديم للقدس وتفتيش بيوتهم.
rawText: إِجْتَوَسَ 8 see 1, in two places. جَاسَ 1 , aor. يَجُوسُ, (S, TA,) inf. n. جَوْسٌ, (A, K,) He sought for, or after, (Zj, S, A, K,) a thing, (Zj, A, K,) or news, or tidings, (S,) with the utmost of his endeavour. (Zj, A, K.) === [Hence,] جَاسُوا خِلَالَ الدِّيَارِ, (S, A,) in the Kur [xvii. 5], (TA,) inf. n. as above, (S, A, K,) They went through the midst of the houses (تَخَلَّلُوهَا), and sought for what was in them, as a man seeks for news, or tidings; as also 🡻اجتاسوا: (S:) or they went to and fro among the houses, in a sudden attack: (A, K:) or the meaning in the Kur is, they slew you amid your houses; and حَاسُوا signifies the same; going and coming: (Fr, TA:) and, inf. n. as above, (K,) and جَوَسَانٌ, (S, K,) they went round about (Zj, S, A, K) by night, (S,) among the houses, (Zj, A, K,) looking if any remained whom they had not slain, (Zj, TA,) or doing mischief: (A:) and 🡻اِجْتِيَاسٌ signifies the same as جَوَسَانٌ, (K, TA,) the going round about by night. (TA.) === جِيسَ also signifies It (anything) was trodden: جَوْسٌ is said to be like دَوْسٌ: (TA:) and you say, جَآءَ فُلَانٌ يَجُوسُ النَّاسَ Such a one came stepping over the people; syn. يَتَخَطَّاهُمْ. (A, TA. *) And accord. to A' Obeyd, جُسْتُهُ and حُسْتُهُ both signify I came into it, and trod it; (خَالَطْتُهُ وَوَطِئْتُهُ;) meaning, any place. (TA.) You say also, جَاسَهُمُ الأَسَدُ The lion trod upon them: or came into the midst of them, and did mischief among them. (TA; [in which the inf. n. of the verb in this sense is said to be جَوْسٌ and جَوْسَآءُ: but the latter is probably a mistranscription for جَوَسَانٌُ.]) جَوَّاسٌ One who treads upon everything: or who comes into the midst of a people, and does mischief among them. (TA.) === Hence, (TA,) The lion. (K, TA.) And in like manner it is applied to a man. (TA.)
